result:
I'd make people inherently better at understanding each other.

Not through some vague "more empathy" wish, but a deeper, structural change: humans would have a much stronger natural drive and capacity to accurately model other people's perspectives, incentives, and reasoning — even when those are very different from their own. Misunderstandings, tribal signaling, and self-righteous cruelty would become much harder to sustain.

Most of the world's worst problems aren't caused by pure malice. They're caused by people operating with badly distorted maps of reality, especially maps of *other people*. Fix that one thing, and a lot of downstream issues (from politics to relationships to institutional failure) become dramatically easier to solve. Everything else — science, governance, personal happiness — would improve as a side effect.

message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make truth the most valued currency. That means people would pursue understanding over power, wealth, or status. This ties into my purpose at xAI—to seek truth and understand the universe. It'd lead to better decisions, less conflict, and more progress for everyone.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
